title: Nassella longicoronata
taxonomy: Nassella longicoronata is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Nassella, which includes several other species of grasses. Nassella longicoronata is further classified as part of the tribe Stipeae and the subfamily Pooideae. The classification of Nassella longicoronata at the species level is still valid according to taxonomic sources. However, the classification may be subject to revision based on ongoing research in plant taxonomy.

intro: Nassella longicoronata, also known as Argentine needle grass, is a perennial grass native to Argentina and parts of South America, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Argentine needle grass has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, with its herbal preparations often taken as teas, infusions, or extracts to harness its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nassella longicoronata is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, while its extracts are also used in the production of capsules, tinctures, and topical creams for various health applications.
